journal_entry.description: Office relocation services provided to customer
journal_entry.rationale: Atlas Van Lines provided relocation services, creating revenue. Since payment is due in 10 days, we record Accounts Receivable (asset increase). Per fix context, office relocation services in Canada are taxable supplies subject to GST/HST. Assuming GST at 5% (conservative rate without province specified), we debit HST Receivable for the tax component and increase the total Accounts Receivable to include both the service amount and tax collected.

output.reason: Atlas Van Lines provided services to a customer for $31,500 with payment due in 10 days. This creates an Accounts Receivable (asset increase) on the debit side, as the company has earned revenue but has not yet received cash. The debit records 1 asset increase (Accounts Receivable).

output.reason: Entry correctly records service revenue on accrual basis with appropriate tax treatment. Balance verified: debit $33,075 = credits $31,500 + $1,575. Accounts Receivable properly debited for total amount including tax. Revenue of $31,500 matches transaction amount. HST calculation correct: $31,500 × 5% = $1,575. HST Payable properly credited as Atlas collected tax from customer. All accounts directionally correct and complete.

approver.reason: Entry correctly records service revenue on accrual basis with appropriate tax treatment. Balance verified: debit $33,075 = credits $31,500 + $1,575. Accounts Receivable properly debited for total amount including tax. Revenue of $31,500 matches transaction amount. HST calculation correct: $31,500 × 5% = $1,575. HST Payable properly credited as Atlas collected tax from customer. All accounts directionally correct and complete.

rejection_reason: Transaction is missing required tax lines. Office relocation services in Canada are taxable supplies subject to GST/HST. The entry records $31,500 but does not include any tax component. The transaction should either: (1) specify that $31,500 is tax-inclusive and break out the tax portion, or (2) add GST/HST on top of $31,500. As written, a taxable service transaction in Canada cannot be recorded without tax lines.

output_entry_builder.description: Office relocation services provided to customer, payment due in 10 days
output_entry_builder.rationale: Atlas Van Lines provided relocation services and will receive payment in 10 days, creating an accounts receivable (asset increase). This is revenue earned under IFRS 15 as the service has been performed. The debit tuple (1,0,0,0,0,0) correctly identifies one asset increase (Accounts Receivable), and the credit tuple (0,0,1,0,0,0) correctly identifies one revenue increase. No tax information was provided, so no HST/GST lines are added (though in practice, relocation services would typically be taxable). The entry recognizes revenue on an accrual basis as the service has been completed.
